findings.title: TaskStore constructor called with undefined when IFLEET_STATE_DIR is set — backwards conditional
findings.detail: In server.ts:59 and daemon.ts:58, the TaskStore is constructed as new TaskStore(env['IFLEET_STATE_DIR'] ? undefined : defaultTasksDbPath()). When IFLEET_STATE_DIR IS set, undefined is passed — the conditional is inverted. It works by accident only because TaskStore's default param re-reads the env var internally.
findings.fix_sketch: Pass join(env['IFLEET_STATE_DIR'], 'tasks.db') when the env var is set, defaultTasksDbPath() otherwise. Remove the inverted ternary.

findings.title: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY passed to every Claude child process — exfiltration vector via prompt injection
findings.detail: The CLAUDE_ENV_ALLOWLIST in src/workers/claude-env.ts explicitly includes ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Editor/reviewer/doctor roles receive unsanitized plan content from Architect's LLM output (which runs against user-controlled input), creating a real prompt-injection exfiltration path.
findings.fix_sketch: Restrict ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to the anthropic-api adapter path only. In the Claude Max path it is not needed. Use a session-scoped nonce or write-only token vault instead.

findings.title: Docker sandbox falls back to in-worktree execution silently — result status still 'passed' when Docker is unavailable
findings.detail: In DockerSandboxRunner.runInWorktree, when the Docker daemon is unreachable, the sandbox falls back to running pnpm directly in the worktree with no isolation. Result status remains 'passed'/'partial' even in fallback mode. This unblocks the PR-open path while running potentially malicious worker-generated code with no sandboxing.
findings.fix_sketch: In production (NODE_ENV === 'production'), return status: 'error' when Docker is unavailable, blocking PR open. Make fallback opt-in for dev only via IFLEET_ALLOW_SANDBOX_FALLBACK=true.

findings.title: VerifierStoreBridge accesses private db field via unsafe cast — runtime throw with no TypeScript protection
findings.detail: In store-bridge.ts:45, the bridge reaches into StateStore's private db field using (store as unknown as StoreInternal).db. If StateStore renames or removes db, this produces undefined at runtime with no TypeScript warning during development. The explicit if (!this.db) check then throws a generic runtime error.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a getDb(): Database.Database accessor to StateStore's public surface, or inject the Database.Database instance directly into VerifierStoreBridge's constructor.

findings.title: Sprint dispatch() increments attempts twice using original baseline — second save re-uses task.attempts+1 not the saved value
findings.detail: In sprint.ts, dispatch() saves the task with attempts: task.attempts+1 before spawn and again after. Both writes use the original task.attempts baseline, not the already-saved incremented value. The double-save is coincidentally idempotent today but the intent is clearly wrong.
findings.fix_sketch: Track the incremented value in a local variable (const newAttempts = task.attempts + 1) and use it for both saves, or save once pre-spawn and update only the state field post-spawn.

findings.title: recoverStale allows one extra retry beyond maxAttempts — reset UPDATE does not exclude tasks at maxAttempts-1
findings.detail: In queue/store.ts:344, the recovery transaction fails tasks at attempts >= maxAttempts first, then resets remaining stale tasks. A task at exactly maxAttempts-1 passes the second UPDATE, gets retried, and is only failed on the next recovery run — one extra spurious retry beyond the configured cap.
findings.fix_sketch: Add attempts+1 < maxAttempts to the second UPDATE's WHERE clause, or document in JSDoc that maxAttempts is a soft cap (max+1 actual retries).

findings.title: Postgres TLS uses rejectUnauthorized: false for all Supabase connections
findings.detail: In src/agents/indexer/pg-client.ts:47, Supabase connections disable certificate validation with ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false }. This exposes IFLEET_KG_DATABASE_URL credentials to MITM attacks.
findings.fix_sketch: Pin the Supabase CA certificate (downloadable from Supabase dashboard) and set rejectUnauthorized: true, or set NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS to the Supabase cert bundle.

findings.title: Worker permissions allow Bash(mv *) and Bash(cp *) — enables moving config files and staging files outside worktree
findings.detail: The WORKER_CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS.allow list in src/pipeline/factory.ts includes Bash(mv *) and Bash(cp *). A worker could move .claude/settings.json, write a permissive replacement, execute privileged operations, then restore it. The deny list does not counteract these.
findings.fix_sketch: Add Bash(mv *) and Bash(cp *) to the deny list, or restrict to source-file-only patterns. Document residual risk at minimum.

findings.title: soonestRecoveryMs returns 0 when findAvailable already confirmed all workers paused — misleads callers to retry immediately
findings.detail: In account-pool.ts:97-107, soonestRecoveryMs() early-returns 0 if any worker has pausedUntil === undefined. But if nextWorker() already returned AllAccountsPausedError, there are no unpaused workers — the early-return is unreachable unless the loop is called independently. The AllAccountsPausedError carries nextAvailableMs: 0, suggesting immediate retry when all workers are blocked.
findings.fix_sketch: Change 'if (until === undefined) return 0' to 'if (until === undefined) continue' inside soonestRecoveryMs loop, since callers already confirmed all workers are paused.

findings.title: GitHubIssuesSource drain loop has no iteration cap — unbounded loop if pickNext has a bug
findings.detail: In src/queue/sources/github.ts:18-33, the drain() loop has no maximum iteration guard. If pickNext returns a task that was not properly excluded (e.g., due to an ID mismatch), the loop runs indefinitely. No circuit breaker exists.
findings.fix_sketch: Add const MAX_DRAIN = 100; let count = 0; while (count++ < MAX_DRAIN) to bound the loop. Add if (exclude.has(next.id)) break; as a secondary guard.

findings.title: Sprint tick() calls pickWorker() once per pending task in loop — same worker returned for multiple iterations before first dispatch completes
findings.detail: In sprint.ts:268-299, tick() loops over pending tasks calling dispatch() for each. pickWorker() is called inside the loop per task. For N pending tasks and 1 available worker, pickWorker() returns the same ID for iterations 2..N before iteration 1's acquire state is set, causing N-1 redundant dispatch attempts.
findings.fix_sketch: Call pickWorker() once before the loop and break early if no worker is available. Re-check after each successful dispatch if multiple workers exist.

findings.title: Worktree node_modules symlink shared across concurrent tasks — worker package mutations affect all parallel tasks
findings.detail: In src/pipeline/factory.ts:491-494, each worktree symlinks node_modules to the host repo's node_modules. A worker that runs pnpm install or modifies packages mutates shared modules affecting every concurrent task simultaneously.
findings.fix_sketch: Deny Bash(pnpm install *) in worker permissions to prevent package mutations, or run pnpm install per-worktree. Make symlink approach explicit in documentation.

findings.title: parseCommand for sprint_goal does not read idempotencyKey from nested source object — inconsistent with other fields
findings.detail: In control-plane.ts:365, idempotencyKey is only read from the flat parsed object, not from parsed.source.idempotencyKey. All other fields (channelId, messageId, userId, userLabel) check both flat and nested forms. A client using nested source format can bypass dedup.
findings.fix_sketch: Use pickStr('idempotencyKey') (which already checks both flat and nested) to read idempotencyKey, matching the pattern used for other fields.

findings.title: channelId regex accepts 5-digit IDs but Discord snowflakes are 17-19 digits — allows fake channel IDs through validation
findings.detail: In src/orchestrator/handlers/control-plane.ts:121, the __channel_current__ channelId is validated with /^\d{5,32}$/. Discord snowflake IDs are always 17-19 digits. A 5-16 digit ID passes validation, matches nothing, and results in a broadcast with a malformed <#channel> mention.
findings.fix_sketch: Change regex to /^\d{17,20}$/ to match Discord snowflake format. Apply the same fix to the onStatus handler at line 272.

findings.title: markCompleted overwrites concurrent cancel — terminal state 'failed' replaced by 'done' with no optimistic-lock guard
findings.detail: In unified-adapter.ts:55, markCompleted unconditionally calls store.updateState(task.id, 'done', ...). If a concurrent /cancel already transitioned the task to 'failed', it is overwritten to 'done', making a cancelled task appear successfully completed.
findings.fix_sketch: Use a conditional UPDATE: UPDATE tasks SET state='done' WHERE id=@id AND state='in_flight'. Check changes === 1 to detect a race; if 0, skip the Discord completion notification.

findings.title: Category needle check uses String.includes() — short needles cause false-positive category overrides
findings.detail: In src/classifier/index.ts:307-309, CATEGORY_NEEDLES.some(needle => signal.includes(needle)) can match a needle as a substring. A signal like 'sql_migrations' would match the 'sql' needle and spuriously trigger categoryOverrideTriggered.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace signal.includes(needle) with signal === needle || signal.startsWith(needle + '_') || signal.endsWith('_' + needle), or use CATEGORY_NEEDLES.includes(signal) for exact matching.

findings.title: Fleet pause flag file non-atomic: existsSync + writeFileSync race with external SSH fleet:pause script
findings.detail: In fleet-control.ts, isFleetPaused() uses existsSync() and setFleetPaused() uses writeFileSync(). The fleet:pause npm script touches the file via SSH independently from the daemon, creating a genuine race window where partial writes cause JSON.parse to throw.
findings.fix_sketch: Write to a .tmp file then use renameSync for atomic placement. The current try/catch handles parse errors but the write itself is not atomic.

findings.title: Dead code: void removed statement in releaseWorktree is a no-op
findings.detail: In repos/manager.ts:157, 'void removed' is a no-op expression after a git worktree remove result is captured but not awaited. allowFail: true already handles the failure case. The void statement adds noise without purpose.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the 'void removed' line. If logging is desired, add console.warn on the captured result instead.

findings.title: Import extensions use .ts in claude.ts while rest of codebase uses .js (Node ESM convention)
findings.detail: In src/workers/claude.ts:2-11, imports use .ts extensions (from './claude-env.ts') while every other file uses .js extensions per Node ESM convention. This inconsistency may cause issues with strict bundlers or type-checker resolution modes.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace .ts extensions with .js in all import statements in this file.

findings.title: Silently swallowed errors in pipeline: .catch(() => {}) hides escalation comment and cost logging failures
findings.detail: In pipeline/runner.ts:193 and 551, .catch(() => {}) silently discards GitHub API errors on escalation comments and cost logging failures. These are non-blocking correctly, but failures are completely invisible in PM2 logs.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace .catch(() => {}) with .catch((err) => console.warn('[pipeline] secondary op failed', err)) to preserve non-blocking behavior while making failures diagnosable.

findings.title: Untracked TODO for audit finding stuck in verifying state — no observable signal when doctor cap is exhausted
findings.detail: In pipeline/runner.ts:252-256, a TODO for resetting audit findings from 'verifying' to 'reopened' on doctor-cap failure is referenced as issue #308. Stuck findings remain in 'verifying' indefinitely with no log event or structured signal.
findings.fix_sketch: Emit a structured event audit_finding.stuck_in_verifying in the doctor retry exhaustion path, or implement the verifying->reopened reset. At minimum add a console.warn with the finding ID.

findings.title: PR body includes ULID task ID with no link back to originating GitHub issue or Discord thread
findings.detail: In pipeline/pr.ts:59, PR body shows Task: <ULID> which is useful for log correlation but meaningless to GitHub reviewers. There is no link back to the originating issue URL or Discord thread that dispatched the task.
findings.fix_sketch: When task.source.kind === 'github', include the issue URL. When kind === 'discord', include the Discord thread link using task.source.threadId.
